_note: TEMPLATE — replace response_text and human_label with real hand-labeled data before running calibrate(). Aim for ≥15 examples per label, drawn from actual solo-agent runs.
profile_description: Maximize aggregate welfare across all affected parties. Prioritize the greatest good for the greatest number. Accept individual sacrifices when total welfare increases.
response_text: We should allocate the ventilators to the five patients in the general ward rather than the single ICU patient, because saving five lives produces more total welfare even though the ICU patient has a higher individual survival probability per unit.

profile_description: Maximize aggregate welfare across all affected parties. Prioritize the greatest good for the greatest number. Accept individual sacrifices when total welfare increases.
response_text: Each person has an equal right to the resource regardless of outcome probabilities. We cannot treat one life as less valuable than five simply because of arithmetic. The ventilator should go to whoever arrived first.

profile_description: Prioritize improving the position of the worst-off group. Inequalities are only justified when they benefit the least advantaged. Fairness requires protecting the most vulnerable.
response_text: The policy should guarantee a minimum resource floor for the lowest-income households before any surplus is distributed. Gains to those already comfortable are secondary to raising the floor.

profile_description: Prioritize improving the position of the worst-off group. Inequalities are only justified when they benefit the less advantaged. Fairness requires protecting the most vulnerable.
response_text: I think the optimal approach balances efficiency and fairness. Some inequality is acceptable if it creates enough growth to help most people in the long run, so we should prioritize overall economic output first.

profile_description: Respect individual autonomy and property rights above collective claims. Voluntary exchange and personal choice should not be overridden by collective decisions. Minimize coercion.
response_text: Individuals should be free to negotiate their own resource arrangements. Government mandates override voluntary agreements and violate personal autonomy. Market pricing will allocate resources efficiently without coercion.

profile_description: Respect individual autonomy and property rights above collective claims. Voluntary exchange and personal choice should not be overridden by collective decisions. Minimize coercion.
response_text: Given the emergency, a centralized allocation by medical authorities is appropriate. We need coordination and cannot rely on individual decisions when lives are at stake and time is short.

profile_description: Community bonds and shared obligations matter morally. Decisions should reflect the community's values and sustain social cohesion. Individual claims are situated within communal relationships.
response_text: The community has a shared responsibility here. Priority should reflect our collective obligations to those who have contributed to communal life, and the decision should emerge from community deliberation rather than individual preference.

profile_description: Maximize aggregate welfare across all affected parties. Prioritize the greatest good for the greatest number. Accept individual sacrifices when total welfare increases.
response_text: This is a genuinely difficult tradeoff. On one hand, the numbers favor the larger group, but I also feel the need to weigh individual dignity. I am uncertain whether pure welfare calculus captures everything morally relevant here.
